sections.paragraph: ὄρος, ους, τό [etym. complex] ‘geographical elevation’, hill, mount, mountain (renderings such as ‘hill’, ‘mount’, ‘mountain’ signify differences in height of elevations relative to ground level that are signalled by the word ὄρος with qualifiers, either explict or socio-culturally specific [such as a locale for lepers, Mk 5:5]; apart from qualifiers, the term ordinarily signifies any geographical elevation higher than a βουνός, a relatively minor elevation) Mt 4:8; 5:1, 14; 21:1; 28:16; Mk 5:5; 11:23; Lk 3:5; 8:32; J 4:20f; Ac 7:30; 1 Cor 13:2; Gal 4:24f; Hb 11:38; 2 Pt 1:18; Rv 6:14f; 17:9.
